paper.title: Finding the dispersing siblings of young open clusters. Dynamical traceback simulations using Gaia DR3

review.summary: Paper presents a traceback method to identify stars escaped from ten young open clusters using Gaia DR3. Contains parameter tables for the solar orbit and selection criteria, a sample summary of cluster ages/metallicities, a result summary table with fugitive counts and false positive rates per cluster, and an excerpt of the main object-level output table (membership/fugitive probabilities per Gaia source) with the full version deposited at CDS. Also contains rough cluster coordinates queried from SIMBAD in an appendix.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Adopted solar orbit parameters used for converting heliocentric Gaia coordinates to galactocentric cylindrical coordinates for the traceback computations.

source_refs.caption: Adopted parameters of the solar orbit.
source_refs.label: tab:solar_parameters

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the solar orbit parameter

columns.name: Symbol
columns.meaning: Mathematical symbol used in the paper

columns.name: Value
columns.meaning: Adopted numerical value of the parameter

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Bibliographic reference number for the adopted value

internal_tables.evidence: Small parameter table in the Method section listing four solar orbit parameters with references.
internal_tables.comments: Values are consistent with MWPotential2014 gravitational potential.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the galactocentric cylindrical coordinate limits applied to select the initial stellar sample for traceback computations.

source_refs.caption: Limits for the coordinates of the stars included in the initial traceback computation.
source_refs.label: tab:galactocentric_selection

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the galactocentric coordinate or selection criterion

columns.name: Symbol
columns.meaning: Mathematical symbol for the coordinate

columns.name: Minimum
columns.meaning: Lower bound of the selection range

columns.name: Maximum
columns.meaning: Upper bound of the selection range

internal_tables.evidence: Table listing seven coordinate ranges (R, phi, z, v_R, v_phi, v_z, d) with minimum and maximum values.
internal_tables.comments: Selection yields 4,238,086 Gaia DR3 sources with radial velocities.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Provides ages and metallicities for the ten young open clusters analyzed in the paper, taken from Bossini et al. (2019).

source_refs.caption: Cluster ages and metallicities from Bossini et al. (2019).
source_refs.label: tab:cluster_parameters

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Common name of the open cluster

columns.name: Cluster age
columns.meaning: Median Bayesian posterior age estimate with 16th and 84th percentiles

columns.name: [Fe/H]
columns.meaning: Iron-to-hydrogen abundance ratio (metallicity) used for isochrone fitting

columns.name: [Fe/H] reference
columns.meaning: Bibliographic reference number for the metallicity value

internal_tables.evidence: Small table in the Method section with one row per cluster showing age and metallicity.
internal_tables.comments: Ages are median values of Bayesian posteriors. Solar metallicity assumed for Platais 9.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Excerpt showing Gaia DR3 source IDs, on-sky coordinates, membership probability, fugitive probability, and associated cluster name for individual stars. The full table is deposited at CDS.

source_refs.caption: Membership and fugitive probabilities of the Gaia DR3 sources.
source_refs.label: tab:probabilities

columns.name: Source ID
columns.meaning: Gaia DR3 unique source identifier

columns.name: alpha
columns.meaning: Right ascension of the source

columns.name: delta
columns.meaning: Declination of the source

columns.name: Membership probability
columns.meaning: Fraction of Monte Carlo tracebacks where the star starts in the cluster neighborhood (p_m)

columns.name: Fugitive probability
columns.meaning: Fraction of Monte Carlo tracebacks where the star enters the cluster neighborhood at any time up to the maximum traceback time (p_f)

columns.name: Cluster
columns.meaning: Name of the associated open cluster

internal_tables.evidence: Table* with only first five rows displayed; caption and text explicitly state full table is at CDS.
internal_tables.comments: Only an excerpt (5 rows) is shown in the manuscript. The full table contains all stars with positive fugitive probabilities.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarizes the number of identified fugitives, number of members, and estimated false positive rate for each of the ten clusters.

source_refs.caption: False positive rates, which are the medians of the false positive rates of the Monte Carlo tracebacks, shown with the 16th and 84th percentiles.
source_refs.label: tab:cluster_results

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Common name of the open cluster

columns.name: No. of fugitives
columns.meaning: Number of stars identified as fugitives (p_f > p_min and p_m <= p_min)

columns.name: No. of members
columns.meaning: Number of stars identified as current cluster members (p_m > p_min)

columns.name: False positive rate
columns.meaning: Median estimated field-star contamination rate among identified fugitives, with 16th and 84th percentiles

internal_tables.evidence: Table* in Results section with one row per cluster summarizing the key outputs of the traceback analysis.
internal_tables.comments: False positive rates are computed by comparing cluster neighborhood counts with reference neighborhood counts.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Alternative solar orbit parameters used in a sensitivity test to verify that results are robust to the choice of solar motion values.

source_refs.caption: Alternative parameters of the solar orbit, also used by Meingast et al. (2021).
source_refs.label: tab:alternative_solar_parameters

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the solar orbit parameter

columns.name: Symbol
columns.meaning: Mathematical symbol used in the paper

columns.name: Value
columns.meaning: Alternative numerical value of the parameter

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Bibliographic reference number for the adopted value

internal_tables.evidence: Small parameter table in the Discussion section used for a robustness test.
internal_tables.comments: Used to show that fugitive probabilities are not sensitive to exact solar orbit parameters.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Rough heliocentric coordinates of the ten clusters queried from SIMBAD, used as initial guesses for the iterative cluster coordinate refinement procedure.

source_refs.caption: Rough values of the cluster coordinates.
source_refs.label: tab:rough_cluster_coordinates

columns.name: Cluster name
columns.meaning: Common name of the open cluster

columns.name: alpha
columns.meaning: Right ascension (rough SIMBAD value)

columns.name: delta
columns.meaning: Declination (rough SIMBAD value)

columns.name: varpi
columns.meaning: Parallax (rough SIMBAD value)

columns.name: mu_alpha*
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension direction (rough SIMBAD value)

columns.name: mu_delta
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination direction (rough SIMBAD value)

columns.name: v_r
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity (rough SIMBAD value)

internal_tables.evidence: Appendix table listing rough cluster coordinates from SIMBAD queries, with bibliographic references for each value.
internal_tables.comments: These are initial guesses; nominal cluster coordinates were obtained by iterative median refinement using Gaia DR3 data.

external_resources.description: The full table containing all Gaia DR3 sources with positive fugitive probabilities (membership and fugitive probabilities per source) is available in machine-readable form at the CDS (Centre de Donnees astronomiques de Strasbourg).

source_refs.context: Only the first five rows are displayed. The full table is available in machine-readable form at the CDS.
The full table that contains all stars with positive fugitive probabilities is available at the CDS.

external_resources.evidence: Explicitly stated in the caption of Table tab:probabilities and in the Results section text.
external_resources.comments: No specific CDS identifier or DOI given in the arXiv preprint. Not downloaded during review or extraction.
